scope: timestamp bumps on unchanged content across graph-metadata.json, description.json, and the global cache
summary: Graph metadata is already idempotent via graphMetadataEqualIgnoringVolatile plus a no-op skip. Description.json and the global cache still stamp wall-clock time and write unconditionally, so reruns dirty unchanged folders.

title: Make description.json writes content-hash gated
problem: generatePerFolderDescription stamps lastUpdated with new Date on every generation and savePerFolderDescription writes unconditionally. So generate-description.js reruns dirty unrelated unchanged folders.
evidence: folder-discovery.ts:893-898 lastUpdated new Date, savePerFolderDescription :962-980 unconditional write, workflow.ts:1714-1720 bumps lastUpdated on every save
proposal: Compute a stable fingerprint from spec.md content, normalized folder identity, authored fields, memorySequence, memoryNameHistory. If it matches the existing file, preserve lastUpdated and skip the write. Only canonical saves with real content changes advance timestamps.

title: Hash-gate the global descriptions.json cache
problem: generateFolderDescriptions sets cache generated from new Date and ensureDescriptionCache writes the regenerated cache, so a refresh creates scoped-commit noise just because another session touched a folder timestamp.
evidence: folder-discovery.ts:674-750 generated new Date, ensureDescriptionCache writes :1153-1179, getSpecsBasePaths includes both roots :1075-1083
proposal: Add a deterministic folders_hash for the sorted entries and write descriptions.json only when that hash changes. Preserve generated on no-op regenerations.

title: Persist a source fingerprint for graph-metadata.json
problem: deriveGraphMetadata stamps last_save_at from nowIso on every derive. The write path already partly solves this with graphMetadataEqualIgnoringVolatile and a no-op return, so the idempotency rule is inferred from output comparison rather than explicit.
evidence: graph-metadata-parser.ts:1094 and :1115 nowIso stamp, no-op skip :1275-1286, regression test graph-metadata-refresh.vitest.ts:148-160
proposal: Add a persisted derived.source_fingerprint or content_hash from ordered canonical source-doc paths plus bytes, reusing content-id.ts:19-24. If it matches, return existing file bytes before deriving volatile fields. P1 because graph no-op writes are already partly solved.

title: Separate access and freshness telemetry from generated JSON
problem: last_accessed_at is schema-required and preserved by refresh, and phase-parent pointer updates directly mutate last_save_at, so reads and resumes can dirty metadata files.
evidence: graph-metadata-schema.ts:51, derive preserve graph-metadata-parser.ts:1117, generate-context.ts:552-588 mutates last_save_at
proposal: Keep access and freshness events in the DB or index layer rather than generated repo JSON. Generated JSON should change only when source docs or structural relationships change.
